unitTitle: Unit 2: Past simple, continuous & used to
unitSubtitle: Finished past actions, actions in progress in the past, and past habits with used to

sections.title: Use
items: Actions or events finished in the past
Things that happened again and again in the past
Situations that existed in the past but no longer do
The key events that move a story forward

sections.title: Examples
items: We **moved** to a new house last spring.
As a child, she **visited** her grandparents every Sunday.
People **didn't use** smartphones thirty years ago.
He **grabbed** his bag, **ran** down the stairs and **jumped** into the car.

sections.title: Useful words
items: yesterday
last week/summer/year/etc
in January/2001/etc
an hour/a week/a year ago

sections.title: Be careful
items: Some verbs have irregular past simple forms.

cards.title: Past continuous
cards.subtitle: Actions in progress in the past

sections.title: Use
items: Describing what was happening at a specific time in the past
Two activities going on simultaneously in the past
Setting the scene at the start of a story

sections.title: Examples
items: At eight o'clock this morning, Dad **was making** breakfast.
She **was talking** on the phone while her brother **was playing** video games.
The sun **was shining** and the birds **were singing** — it was a perfect day.

sections.title: Useful words
items: at that moment
at one/two/etc o'clock
while

sections.title: Be careful
items: When a shorter action interrupts a longer one in the past, combine the past simple and past continuous: I **dropped** my keys while I **was running** to the bus.
Do not use the past continuous for regular or repeated actions in the past.

sections.title: Use
items: Past habits or states that no longer exist

sections.title: Example
items: My grandfather **used to** walk five kilometres to school when he was young.

formatDefinitions.displayName: Build the sentence with visual tiles
formatDefinitions.pedagogicalGoal: The learner answers a meaningful visual/context question by building the sentence, instead of translating a slash prompt.

formatDefinitions.displayName: Choose the verb tile and conjugate it

interactiveFormatSystem.goal: Reusable interactive exercise screens for grammar practice.

screenTypes.purpose: Test-style choice: choose the correct conjugated form.
Write only the missing conjugated form inside a sentence gap.
See a full sentence with the wrong conjugation marked and type the replacement.
Choose a base verb from word tiles, then write it correctly conjugated in the gap.
Find wrong conjugations in a text one at a time, then write each correction.
studentFlow: Tap a wrong verb phrase and press Check
Press Continue to confirm the mark and advance the progress bar
Write the correction for that error
Press Continue to see the fix in green, then find the next error

screenTypes.purpose: Build a sentence by ordering word tiles, with extra distractors.

practiceNodes.title: Simple or continuous?
focus: completed action vs action in progress

hintPrompts: Is this a finished action or an action in progress?
Do you need past simple or past continuous?
Is it a past habit that is different now?
Do you need did, was or were?

exercises.exerciseTypeName: Choose the Verb Tile and Conjugate It
exercises.title: A: Past simple (verbs in the box)
exercises.instructions: Complete using the correct past simple form of the verbs in the box. You may have to use some negative forms.
exercises.studentInstruction: Tap a verb tile, then type the past simple form.

items.sentence: I was thirsty, so Dad ...... me a bottle of water.
items.explanation: Use the past simple because the action is finished in the past.
items.template: I was thirsty, so Dad {gap1} me a bottle of water.
items.blankSentence: I was thirsty, so Dad ____ me a bottle of water.

items.sentence: We waited for Alex for an hour, but he ...... .
items.explanation: Use did not / didn't + base verb for a negative action in the past.
items.template: We waited for Alex for an hour, but he {gap1} .
items.blankSentence: We waited for Alex for an hour, but he ____ .

items.sentence: Sarah ...... a really interesting article for the school magazine.
items.explanation: Use the past simple because the action is finished in the past.
items.template: Sarah {gap1} a really interesting article for the school magazine.
items.blankSentence: Sarah ____ a really interesting article for the school magazine.

items.sentence: Nobody ...... the answer to the teacher's question.
items.explanation: Use the past simple because the action is finished in the past.
items.template: Nobody {gap1} the answer to the teacher's question.
items.blankSentence: Nobody ____ the answer to the teacher's question.

items.sentence: Tom ...... his wallet under the sofa yesterday.
items.explanation: Use the past simple because the action is finished in the past.
items.template: Tom {gap1} his wallet under the sofa yesterday.
items.blankSentence: Tom ____ his wallet under the sofa yesterday.

items.sentence: My uncle ...... us to the airport early in the morning.
items.explanation: Use the past simple because the action is finished in the past.
items.template: My uncle {gap1} us to the airport early in the morning.
items.blankSentence: My uncle ____ us to the airport early in the morning.

items.sentence: Last summer, we ...... some new friends at camp.
items.explanation: Use the past simple because the action is finished in the past.
items.template: Last summer, we {gap1} some new friends at camp.
items.blankSentence: Last summer, we ____ some new friends at camp.

items.sentence: I was cold, so I ...... a new jacket.
items.explanation: Use the past simple because the action is finished in the past.
items.template: I was cold, so I {gap1} a new jacket.
items.blankSentence: I was cold, so I ____ a new jacket.

exercises.title: B: Match to make sentences (past simple)
exercises.instructions: Match each sentence beginning (1–6) with the correct ending (A–F).
exercises.studentInstruction: Tap the ending that completes the sentence correctly.
exercises.grammarFocus: past simple reasons and finished actions

items.originalSentence: I don't want another sandwich because **D** I ate lunch an hour ago.
items.originalAnswer: I ate lunch an hour ago.
items.sentenceBefore: I don't want another sandwich because
options: I ate lunch an hour ago.
items.answer: I ate lunch an hour ago.
items.completedSentence: I don't want another sandwich because I ate lunch an hour ago.
items.explanation: The ending gives the past reason that explains the first part of the sentence.
items.template: I don't want another sandwich because {choice}

items.originalSentence: I don't need your bike because **F** I bought one last month.
items.sentenceBefore: I don't need your bike because
items.completedSentence: I don't need your bike because I bought one last month.
items.explanation: The ending gives the past reason that explains the first part of the sentence.
items.template: I don't need your bike because {choice}

items.originalSentence: I know a lot about Rome because **A** I visited it last year.
items.sentenceBefore: I know a lot about Rome because
items.completedSentence: I know a lot about Rome because I visited it last year.
items.explanation: The ending gives the past reason that explains the first part of the sentence.
items.template: I know a lot about Rome because {choice}

items.originalSentence: I don't have to clean my room now because **E** I tidied it yesterday.
items.sentenceBefore: I don't have to clean my room now because
items.completedSentence: I don't have to clean my room now because I tidied it yesterday.
items.explanation: The ending gives the past reason that explains the first part of the sentence.
items.template: I don't have to clean my room now because {choice}

items.originalSentence: I can't play that computer game any more because **B** I lost it last weekend.
items.sentenceBefore: I can't play that computer game any more because
items.completedSentence: I can't play that computer game any more because I lost it last weekend.
items.explanation: The ending gives the past reason that explains the first part of the sentence.
items.template: I can't play that computer game any more because {choice}

items.originalSentence: Dad is upset with me because **C** I forgot his birthday.
items.sentenceBefore: Dad is upset with me because
options: I ate lunch an hour ago.
items.completedSentence: Dad is upset with me because I forgot his birthday.
items.explanation: The ending gives the past reason that explains the first part of the sentence.
items.template: Dad is upset with me because {choice}

exercises.title: C: Past continuous
exercises.instructions: Complete using the correct past continuous form of the verbs in brackets.
exercises.studentInstruction: Type only the missing past continuous form.

items.sentence: Mia ...... (read) a magazine at nine o'clock.
items.explanation: Use the past continuous for an action in progress at a moment in the past.
items.template: Mia {gap1} a magazine at nine o'clock.
items.blankSentence: Mia ____ a magazine at nine o'clock.

items.sentence: At midnight, we ...... (drive) home while it ...... (snow).
items.explanation: Use the past continuous for an action in progress at a moment in the past.
items.template: At midnight, we {gap1} home while it {gap2}.
items.blankSentence: At midnight, we ____ home while it ____.

items.sentence: Ben ...... (wait) outside the cinema when his friends arrived.
items.explanation: Use the past continuous for an action in progress at a moment in the past.
items.template: Ben {gap1} outside the cinema when his friends arrived.
items.blankSentence: Ben ____ outside the cinema when his friends arrived.

items.sentence: I saw Olivia while she ...... (shop) in the town centre.
items.explanation: Use the past continuous for an action in progress at a moment in the past.
items.template: I saw Olivia while she {gap1} in the town centre.
items.blankSentence: I saw Olivia while she ____ in the town centre.

items.sentence: Ella ...... (cycle) to school when it started raining.
items.explanation: Use the past continuous for an action in progress at a moment in the past.
items.template: Ella {gap1} to school when it started raining.
items.blankSentence: Ella ____ to school when it started raining.

items.sentence: When you called Harry, ...... he ...... (sleep)?
items.explanation: Use the past continuous for an action in progress at a moment in the past.
items.template: When you called Harry, {gap1} he {gap2}?
items.blankSentence: When you called Harry, ____ he ____?

items.sentence: At ten o'clock last night, we ...... (study) for the exam.
items.explanation: Use the past continuous for an action in progress at a moment in the past.
items.template: At ten o'clock last night, we {gap1} for the exam.
items.blankSentence: At ten o'clock last night, we ____ for the exam.

exercises.title: D: Circle the correct word or phrase
exercises.instructions: Circle the correct word or phrase.

items.sentenceBefore: When we were in Scotland, we
items.completedSentence: When we were in Scotland, we walked in the mountains every day.
items.originalSentence: When we were in Scotland, we **walked / were walking** in the mountains every day.
items.explanation: Choose past simple for completed events and past continuous for actions in progress or background situations.
items.template: When we were in Scotland, we {choice} in the mountains every day.

items.completedSentence: A few years ago, I started learning Japanese.
items.originalSentence: A few years ago, I **started / was starting** learning Japanese.
items.explanation: Choose past simple for completed events and past continuous for actions in progress or background situations.
items.template: A few years ago, I {choice} learning Japanese.

items.completedSentence: Emma was making dinner when the guests arrived.
items.originalSentence: Emma **made / was making** dinner when the guests arrived.
items.explanation: Choose past simple for completed events and past continuous for actions in progress or background situations.
items.template: Emma {choice} dinner when the guests arrived.

items.completedSentence: We hurried home because it was snowing heavily.
items.originalSentence: We hurried home because it **snowed / was snowing** heavily.
items.explanation: Choose past simple for completed events and past continuous for actions in progress or background situations.
items.template: We hurried home because it {choice} heavily.

items.sentenceAfter: in the playground, so a teacher stopped them.
items.completedSentence: Two children were fighting in the playground, so a teacher stopped them.
items.originalSentence: Two children **fought / were fighting** in the playground, so a teacher stopped them.
items.explanation: Choose past simple for completed events and past continuous for actions in progress or background situations.
items.template: Two children {choice} in the playground, so a teacher stopped them.

items.sentenceAfter: me a text message yesterday afternoon.
items.completedSentence: Chris sent me a text message yesterday afternoon.
items.originalSentence: Chris **sent / was sending** me a text message yesterday afternoon.
items.explanation: Choose past simple for completed events and past continuous for actions in progress or background situations.
items.template: Chris {choice} me a text message yesterday afternoon.

items.sentenceAfter: dinner when somebody knocked at the door.
items.completedSentence: We were having dinner when somebody knocked at the door.
items.originalSentence: We **had / were having** dinner when somebody knocked at the door.
items.explanation: Choose past simple for completed events and past continuous for actions in progress or background situations.
items.template: We {choice} dinner when somebody knocked at the door.

items.sentenceBefore: As I passed the kitchen, I saw Mum
items.completedSentence: As I passed the kitchen, I saw Mum was baking a cake.
items.originalSentence: As I passed the kitchen, I saw Mum **baked / was baking** a cake.
items.explanation: Choose past simple for completed events and past continuous for actions in progress or background situations.
items.template: As I passed the kitchen, I saw Mum {choice} a cake.

items.completedSentence: I was sleeping when the storm began.
items.originalSentence: I **slept / was sleeping** when the storm began.
items.explanation: Choose past simple for completed events and past continuous for actions in progress or background situations.
items.template: I {choice} when the storm began.

items.sentenceAfter: his homework, his sister listened to music.
items.completedSentence: While Jake was doing his homework, his sister listened to music.
items.originalSentence: While Jake **did / was doing** his homework, his sister listened to music.
items.explanation: Choose past simple for completed events and past continuous for actions in progress or background situations.
items.template: While Jake {choice} his homework, his sister listened to music.

exercises.title: E: The School Trip
exercises.instructions: Complete using the correct past simple or past continuous form of the verbs in the box.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete the whole story using the correct past simple or past continuous form of the verbs in the box.
exercises.grammarFocus: past simple and past continuous in a story
exercises.passage:
Last Friday, our class (1) ...... very excited because we were going on a school trip. Some students (2) ...... their bags while others (3) ...... outside the school gate. It (4) ...... lightly, but nobody cared.
At eight o'clock, the bus finally (5) ...... . Everyone (6) ...... onto the bus quickly and the teachers (7) ...... hello to all the students.
While we (8) ...... to the museum, some people (9) ...... games and others (10) ...... loudly at jokes.
When we finally arrived, we (11) ...... around the museum in groups. Suddenly, one boy (12) ...... over while he (13) ...... a sandwich.
Luckily, he wasn't hurt and everyone (14) ...... laughing again a few minutes later.

exercises.title: E: used to
exercises.instructions: Complete using the correct form of used to. You may have to use some negative forms.
exercises.studentInstruction: Type only the missing used to form.

items.sentence: When I was a child, I ...... play outside for hours.
items.explanation: Use used to / didn't use to / Did ... use to for past habits or states that are different now.
items.template: When I was a child, I {gap1} play outside for hours.
items.blankSentence: When I was a child, I ____ play outside for hours.

items.sentence: ...... there ...... be a cinema in this street?
items.explanation: Use used to / didn't use to / Did ... use to for past habits or states that are different now.
items.template: {gap1} there {gap2} be a cinema in this street?
items.blankSentence: ____ there ____ be a cinema in this street?

items.sentence: Sophie works in a hospital now, but she ...... want to be an artist.
items.explanation: Use used to / didn't use to / Did ... use to for past habits or states that are different now.
items.template: Sophie works in a hospital now, but she {gap1} want to be an artist.
items.blankSentence: Sophie works in a hospital now, but she ____ want to be an artist.

items.sentence: I ...... enjoy spicy food, but now I love it.
items.explanation: Use used to / didn't use to / Did ... use to for past habits or states that are different now.
items.template: I {gap1} enjoy spicy food, but now I love it.
items.blankSentence: I ____ enjoy spicy food, but now I love it.

items.sentence: ...... your grandfather ...... wear glasses?
items.explanation: Use used to / didn't use to / Did ... use to for past habits or states that are different now.
items.template: {gap1} your grandfather {gap2} wear glasses?
items.blankSentence: ____ your grandfather ____ wear glasses?

items.sentence: We ...... go camping very often, but now we do every summer.
items.explanation: Use used to / didn't use to / Did ... use to for past habits or states that are different now.
items.template: We {gap1} go camping very often, but now we do every summer.
items.blankSentence: We ____ go camping very often, but now we do every summer.

exercises.title: F: Correct the wrong tenses
exercises.instructions: Mark one wrongly conjugated verb phrase at a time. After each correct mark, write its correction before finding the next error.
exercises.studentInstruction: Find an error, confirm it, then write its correction. Repeat until all 10 are fixed.
exercises.grammarFocus: past simple, past continuous and used to in context
exercises.passage: When I was a child, I use to spend every Saturday at my grandparents' house. While grandma was cooked lunch, grandpa usually was worked in the garden. One afternoon, I was deciding to explore the attic. I was climbing the wooden stairs when I was hearing a strange sound above me. Suddenly, a box was falling from a shelf and papers was scattering everywhere. Grandpa was running upstairs because he was thought something was wrong. Later, grandma was smiled and was gave me a slice of cake.

items.explanation: Use used to for past habits or states that are different now.
While shows an action in progress in the past, so use the past continuous.
Usually describes a repeated past habit, so use the past simple.
This is a finished decision in the past, so use the past simple.
A short completed action interrupts the longer background action, so use the past simple.
Suddenly signals a short completed action, so use the past simple.
This describes a completed result in the past, so use the past simple.
Because introduces the reason for a finished reaction, so use the past simple.
Later introduces a completed action, so use the past simple.
This is a finished action in the past, so use the past simple.

implementationNotes: Adapted from the Unit 1 interactive unit structure so Unit 2 uses theory cards, practice nodes, format definitions and content banks.
Original Unit 2 exercise sentences, answers, word banks and passage have been preserved. Matching and passage-input content are represented with Unit 1-compatible screen formats.
Exercise A now uses the verb-tile flow in practice node 6, and a new error-hunt final challenge mirrors Unit 1's mark-and-correct passage flow.

sourceTransformationNotes.contentPreservation: Original exercise content kept; added IDs, explanations, templates, acceptedAnswers, practice nodes and interaction metadata.
formatAdaptations: Original matching exercise rendered as two_option_choice using the same sentence beginnings and endings.
Original passage-input exercise now uses a full-passage practice screen with all numbered gaps fillable at once.
Original verbs-in-the-box exercise now powers a verb-tile practice node.
Added a final error-hunt passage for contextual past tense correction.
